game_overview: Dune: War for Arrakis is an asymmetric strategy board game based on Frank Herbert's Dune. Players recreate the Desert War, the struggle for control of Arrakis, the only source in the Imperium of the spice melange, fought between House Atreides and House Harkonnen. The Harkonnens start controlling the main populated areas and must destroy Fremen Sietches while harvesting spice. The Atreides wage guerrilla warfare alongside their Fremen allies, working to fulfill ancient prophecies. In a 2-player game, each player leads one faction. In 3-4 player games, players form two teams.
objective: Harkonnen wins by accumulating 10 or more Supremacy Points (gained by destroying Fremen Sietches). Atreides wins by reaching or exceeding specific scores on each of the three Prescience tracks (Kwisatz Haderach, Sand Dwellers, and Jihad), as indicated by a Secret Objective card drawn at the start of the game.

components_overview.name: Game Board
components_overview.description: Two-section board showing the northern hemisphere of Arrakis. Divided into 8 Sectors containing Areas of four types: Mountain, Plateau, Minor Erg, and Desert. Desert areas adjacent to board edges are Deep Desert. Includes Air zones for Harkonnen vehicles, the Supremacy Track, and the Prescience Track.

components_overview.name: The Spice Must Flow Board
components_overview.description: Tracks spice harvesting, Imperium relations, and vehicle placement based on the Imperium marker position.

components_overview.name: Player Dashboards
components_overview.description: Each player has a dashboard showing action die slots, the Regeneration Tank, and reference information for action dice results.

components_overview.name: Harkonnen Leaders
components_overview.description: Baron Harkonnen, Beast Rabban, Feyd-Rautha, Captain Aramsham, Thufir Hawat, and 6 Bashar Leaders. Leaders join Legions and provide combat abilities and special actions.

components_overview.name: Atreides Leaders
components_overview.description: Paul Atreides, Paul-Muad'Dib, Lady Jessica, Reverend Mother Jessica, Stilgar, Chani, Gurney Halleck, Alia, and 12 Naib Leaders. Leaders join Legions and provide combat abilities and special actions.

components_overview.name: Harkonnen Units
components_overview.description: 24 Regular units, 12 Elite units, and 8 Sardaukar units (soldier-fanatics provided by the Emperor). Sardaukar have special combat abilities.

components_overview.name: Atreides Units
components_overview.description: 16 Regular units, 8 Elite units, and 6 Fedaykin units (elite Fremen warriors). Fedaykin have special combat abilities.

components_overview.name: Vehicles
components_overview.description: 6 Ornithopters (flying attack/transport), 8 Harvesters (spice collection), and 3 Carryalls (transport harvesters away from danger). Placed in Air zones each round based on the Imperium marker. All Harkonnen vehicles are returned by end of round.

components_overview.name: Sandworms
components_overview.description: 4 Sandworms and 1 Wild Maker. Sandworms move and attack in desert areas. The Wild Maker is a special Atreides unit.

components_overview.name: Settlement Tokens
components_overview.description: 1 Arrakeen, 1 Carthag (major cities), 4 Pyon Villages, and 8 Sietches (Fremen hidden shelters). Harkonnen gains Supremacy Points by destroying Sietches.

components_overview.name: Wormsign Tokens
components_overview.description: Placed during the Desert Hazards phase and via Desert Power action. Wormsigns trigger sandworm appearances and Coriolis Storms that create desert hazards.

components_overview.name: Ecological Testing Stations
components_overview.description: Placed on designated board locations. Stations are not Settlements -- areas with only stations are considered free for both players. When an Atreides legion enters an area with a station, it is removed and advances the corresponding Prescience marker 1 step.

components_overview.name: Deployment Tokens
components_overview.description: 8 Atreides Garrison, 8 Atreides Deployment, and 12 Harkonnen Garrison Deployment tokens. Atreides tokens are placed face-down to hide unit composition. Used for deploying reinforcements.

components_overview.name: Family Atomics Tokens
components_overview.description: 3 tokens representing the devastating Family Atomics weapons. Available when any Prescience marker reaches step 8. Effects: Splintered Rock (Hagga Basin -- exposed to coriolis storms and sandworms), Hole in the Rock (Imperial Basin -- exposed to coriolis storms and sandworms), and Rimwall (Imperial Basin and Arrakeen -- exposed to coriolis storms and sandworms).

components_overview.name: Prescience Markers
components_overview.description: 3 markers tracking the Atreides player's progress: Kwisatz Haderach (green), Sand Dwellers (yellow), and Jihad (red).

components_overview.name: Imperium Markers
components_overview.description: 3 markers representing relations with the great powers of the Imperium: CHOAM, Spacing Guild, and Landsraad. Position determines vehicle placement and Imperium Bans.

components_overview.name: Supremacy Marker
components_overview.description: Tracks Harkonnen Supremacy Points on the Supremacy Track. Harkonnen wins at 10+ SP.

components_overview.name: Spice Reserve Token
components_overview.description: Tracks the current spice reserve level.

components_overview.name: Bene Gesserit Tokens
components_overview.description: Atreides starts with 1 token. Additional tokens gained from Supremacy Track symbols and from Gaius Helen Mohiam entering play. Tokens are placed on dashboards at the start of Action Resolution and act as temporary action dice for that round. Once used, they are discarded from the game.

components_overview.name: Atreides Action Dice
components_overview.description: 4 dice with faces showing: Strategy (move/attack), Leadership (move/attack with leader), Deployment (place deployment token at sietch), Mentat (draw planning cards), House (use as any result), and Desert Power (wormsign/sandworm).

components_overview.name: Harkonnen Action Dice
components_overview.description: Up to 8 dice, number available each round depends on the Spice Must Flow board. Faces show: Strategy (move/attack), Leadership (move/attack with leader), House (replace units or place vehicles), and Mentat (draw planning cards).

components_overview.name: Combat Dice
components_overview.description: 6 Atreides and 6 Harkonnen combat dice. Faces show Hit (dagger), Shield, and Special results.

components_overview.name: Atreides Planning Cards
components_overview.description: 36 cards split into House Atreides deck (18) and Fremen Ally deck (18). Draw 2 per round (1 from each, or 2 from same deck via Mentat). Can be played by spending an action die instead of using the die's action.

components_overview.name: Harkonnen Planning Cards
components_overview.description: 36 cards split into House Harkonnen deck (18) and Corrino Ally deck (18). Draw 2 per round. Can be played by spending an action die.

components_overview.name: Atreides Leader Cards
components_overview.description: 10 cards: Paul Atreides, Lady Jessica, Reverend Mother Jessica, Gurney Halleck, Alia (House Atreides); Naib Leaders, Paul-Muad'Dib, Stilgar, Chani, Wild Maker (Fremen Ally). Define special abilities for each leader.

components_overview.name: Harkonnen Leader Cards
components_overview.description: 6 cards: Beast Rabban, Thufir Hawat, Baron Harkonnen, Feyd-Rautha (House Harkonnen); Bashar Leaders, Captain Aramsham (Corrino Ally). Define special abilities for each leader.

components_overview.name: Prescience Cards
components_overview.description: 16 cards revealed 3 at a time each round. Each card specifies the phase and conditions required to claim it. The Atreides can claim a maximum of 2 per round. Unclaimed cards can be discarded or reshuffled at end of round.

components_overview.name: Objective Cards
components_overview.description: 6 secret objective cards. Atreides draws one at game start -- it defines the Prescience Point thresholds needed on all 3 tracks to win.

components_overview.name: Imperium Bans Cards
components_overview.description: 3 reference cards (CHOAM, Spacing Guild, Landsraad) defining penalties when Imperium markers move down during Spice Harvesting. Effects are described on the corresponding reference cards and apply at all times while active.

setup.description: Assemble the two game board sections and place the Spice Must Flow board nearby.
Place the 8 Sietch Settlement tokens face down on their designated board positions. Only the Atreides player may inspect their hidden side at any time.
Place the 6 Ecological Testing Stations face down on their designated board positions. Their hidden side is secret to all players and is only revealed if an Atreides legion enters the area.
Place the Supremacy marker on the 0 space of the Supremacy Track.
Place the 3 Prescience markers on the 0 space of the Prescience Track: Kwisatz Haderach (green), Sand Dwellers (yellow), and Jihad (red).
The Harkonnen player places the Spice Must Flow board next to the Giedi Prime dashboard, lining up the 3 Imperium markers to the highest step.

Each player takes their Player Dashboard and places all named leader cards (marked as in play at the start) on the action boxes matching their action symbols. Set aside named leaders not starting in play. Place generic leader cards and figures (Naib and Bashar) nearby, keeping named leader figures and cards face down.

Each player shuffles their two Planning card decks (House deck and Ally deck) separately and places them face down. Each player then draws 2 Planning cards (1 from each deck) into their hand. Planning cards in hand are kept hidden from opponents unless a card or rule effect states otherwise.
Shuffle all 16 prescience cards into a face-down deck. Draw 3 prescience cards and place them face up next to the deck.
The Atreides player shuffles the 6 Secret Objective cards, draws 1 (setting aside the remaining 5 unseen), and keeps it hidden from the Harkonnen player for the entire game.
Place the 6 Harkonnen settlement tokens (Arrakeen, Carthag, and 4 Pyon Villages) on the indicated areas. Their rank can be inspected at any time by all players.

Place 2 sets of Harkonnen starting deployment tokens and randomly place 1 token of each type face-down in each area containing a Harkonnen settlement. Flip them face-up and replace with the corresponding Harkonnen figures.
Shuffle the Atreides starting deployment tokens face-down and place 1 face-down on each indicated area containing a sietch. Place 1 Naib Leader figure in each area containing a sietch.
Shuffle the Atreides standard deployment tokens and keep them face-down next to the Atreides player.
Place 16 wormsign tokens face-down to form the wormsign token pool.
The Atreides player starts with 1 Bene Gesserit token. The remaining 3 tokens form the Bene Gesserit reserve.

In a 3-4 player game, share factions between teams. Harkonnen: one player controls House Harkonnen (red leader cards) and the other controls the Corrino Ally subfaction (gray leader cards). Atreides: one controls House Atreides subfaction (green leader cards) and the other controls the Fremen Ally subfaction (yellow leader cards). Use the 3-4 player dashboards. Each team also takes 3 Cone of Silence tokens.

gameplay.turn_structure:
1. Draw cards and reveal prescience cards
2. Place vehicles
3. Alternate actions (Atreides first)
4. Resolve desert hazards
5. Harvest spice
6. Check victory and cleanup

actions.name: Start of the Round
actions.description: Prepare for the new round by drawing cards and revealing prescience cards.

steps.description: Each player draws 2 planning cards, one from each of their decks, into their hand.
Draw 3 prescience cards and place them face up in a row next to the deck, visible to all players.

details.short_description: Can the Atreides draw from the Fremen Ally deck during setup draw?
details.description: In a 3-4 player game, the House Atreides player can draw from the Fremen Ally deck instead and pass the card to the Fremen ally (they can look at it first without using a Cone of Silence).

details.short_description: What if a planning card deck is exhausted?
details.description: Do not reshuffle the discards. You can no longer draw cards from that exhausted deck. If both decks are exhausted, you draw no cards.

actions.name: Step 1: Vehicle Placement
actions.description: The Harkonnen player places vehicles (Ornithopters, Harvesters, Carryalls) based on the position of the lowest Imperium marker on the Spice Must Flow board.

steps.description: Find the active row on the Spice Must Flow board (where the lowest Imperium marker is positioned).

Place an action die on each empty slot of the first column on the active row and the rows above it. These dice won't be used this round. Any action die below the active row are then removed from the board and will be available this round.

Place the indicated number of vehicles on the board: Harvesters are placed in free desert areas (1 figure per area), Ornithopters and Carryalls are placed in Air zones (1 figure per zone). If an Air zone is already occupied, remove the current vehicle first.

notes.description: All Harkonnen vehicles are returned at the end of the round. Vehicles never move from their location once placed.
Vehicles are placed in Air zones, which are connected to all Areas within both adjacent Sectors.
Harvesters collect spice during Step 4. Ornithopters provide Scouting and Troop-Transport. Carryalls can rescue Harvesters from sandworm attacks.

details.short_description: What if CHOAM Imperium Ban is active?
details.description: The stacking limit becomes 5 units per area. Any units exceeding this must be immediately removed.

actions.name: Step 2: Action Resolution
actions.description: The main phase of the game. Players roll action dice, allocate them on their dashboards, then alternate taking actions. Atreides goes first. The phase ends when the Harkonnen player has used all their available actions -- the Atreides player loses any remaining actions.

steps.description: Both players roll all their action dice simultaneously.

Allocate dice on player dashboards (max 3 per result on Harkonnen dashboard, max 2 on Atreides). If dice exceed available slots, reassign excess starting with the player with the fewest number on their dashboard. Tied: Harkonnen goes first. New die results are chosen one at a time, alternating, starting with the Harkonnen player.

Starting with Harkonnen, each player may place 1 Bene Gesserit token (if available) as a temporary action die on an empty action box slot of their dashboard. The Atreides player then has the same choice.

Players alternate taking 1 action each, starting with the Atreides player. On your turn: spend 1 die to execute its action, OR spend any 1 action die to play a planning card from your hand (apply the card effects, then discard it to a discard pile).
If you have 2 or more options on your dashboard, choose only 1. If the Atreides player has fewer unused action dice than the Harkonnen player, they may take a Desert Power action instead.

notes.description: Desert Power (Atreides only): Does not require spending a die. Can only be performed if the Atreides has fewer unused dice than Harkonnen, or as part of a planning card effect or special action. Place 2 wormsign tokens in any desert areas without a wormsign, sandworm, or sietch (1 per area), OR attack with 1 sandworm on the board.

Free Actions can be taken in addition to your main action and do not cost dice. They can be performed at any time during your turn, multiple times, and in addition to (not instead of) normal actions, even interrupting them.
The effects of an action are never mandatory. They can be carried out fully, partially, or even not at all.
Once a Bene Gesserit token is used, it is discarded and removed from play permanently.

details.short_description: Can Atreides act after Harkonnen uses their last die?
details.description: No. The Action Resolution phase is over once the Harkonnen player has executed all their available Actions. The Atreides player loses any remaining Actions.

details.short_description: When both players must change dice results (exceeding slots), what order?
details.description: The new die results are chosen one at a time, in turns, starting with the Harkonnen player.

details.short_description: Can Free Actions interrupt a normal Action?
details.description: Yes. Free Actions can be performed at any time during a player's turn, even interrupting a normal Action.

details.short_description: What happens to Bene Gesserit tokens after they have been used?
details.description: Bene Gesserit tokens are discarded and removed from play as soon as they are used.

details.short_description: If all Generic Leaders are on the board, can a Named Leader still be replaced?
details.description: The Named Leader can still be removed, but is not replaced with any Generic Leader.

actions.name: Step 3: Desert Hazards
actions.description: Wormsign tokens are placed and resolved, then Coriolis Storms sweep the board. The desert strikes back.

steps.description: The Atreides player places random wormsign tokens face-down on the board without looking at them, 1 in each desert area that contains a Harkonnen harvester or legion and that does not already contain a wormsign token or sandworm.

Flip all wormsign tokens on the board face-up and resolve their effects in this order: Sand (false alarm -- discard the token), Sandworm (place a sandworm there, then discard the token), Burrowing Sandworm (if in a deep desert area, place a sandworm there then discard; otherwise discard the token).

Each sandworm appearing must be placed, either from off the board or by relocating from somewhere. If the Wild Maker is in play, the Atreides player can place its figure instead of a normal sandworm. Maximum 4 sandworms on the board (5 if Wild Maker is in play).
After all wormsign tokens have been resolved, shuffle them back face-down into the wormsign pool.

Resolve Coriolis Storms: all Plateau, Minor Erg, and Desert areas on the board are exposed. For each Harkonnen legion in such an area, the Atreides player rolls 2 combat dice. The attack inflicts 1 Hit on the enemy legion for each Hit result, and a variable number of Hits for each Special result based on terrain type.

notes.description: Mountain areas are completely safe from sandworms and Coriolis Storms.
Coriolis Storm Special result hits by terrain: Deep Desert = 2 hits, Desert = 1 hit, Minor Erg or Plateau = no hits. Atreides legions are unaffected by Coriolis Storms.

If a wormsign area contains only a harvester (no legion), the sandworm attacks the harvester. Carryalls can rescue harvesters -- remove a carryall from a connected Air zone to save the harvester (it is not removed and stays in the area, unless attacked by a legion).

If a sandworm attacks an area containing an enemy legion, the Atreides player rolls 4 combat dice for a regular sandworm or 6 for a Wild Maker, inflicting 1 Hit on the enemy legion for each Hit, and Hits for each Special based on terrain type.
After a sandworm eliminates all enemy units in an area and the area contains a harvester, the harvester is also removed (unless a carryall saves it).

details.short_description: Does the Wild Maker increase the sandworm limit to 5?
details.description: Yes to both -- it increases the limit and can be placed during the Desert Hazards phase instead of a normal Sandworm.

details.short_description: Does a Harkonnen Legion retreat when a Sandworm appears?
details.description: Harkonnen Legions never retreat 'during a battle', but they can (and must) retreat when a Sandworm appears -- which does not trigger a battle.

actions.name: Step 4: Spice Harvesting
actions.description: Harvesters collect spice, which determines Imperium relations. The Harkonnen player spends spice points to maintain or raise Imperium marker positions.

steps.description: The Harkonnen player removes harvesters still on the board to collect spice. The amount depends on the area: Desert = 1 spice point, Deep Desert = 2 spice points.

The Harkonnen player immediately spends collected spice points to keep Imperium markers on their current position, or to raise them up 1 step: It costs 2 points to keep a marker in position, and 3 points to raise a marker up 1 step. Each marker can only be raised a maximum of 1 step during the same harvesting phase.
Markers that are neither kept in place nor raised automatically move down 1 step. This causes the activation of Imperium Bans.

Activate ALL Bans corresponding to any Imperium markers on the bottom step of the Spice Must Flow board, plus ALL bans corresponding to markers that just moved down. A ban remains active until the beginning of the next round's harvesting phase.
Return all Harkonnen vehicles (Ornithopters, Harvesters, Carryalls) from the board.

notes.description: CHOAM Ban: Harkonnen stacking limit is reduced from 6 to 5 units per area. Any exceeding units must be immediately removed.
Landsraad Ban: As long as this ban is active, the Harkonnen player cannot discard Planning cards from their hand to gain additional combat dice.

When a ban is active, the Harkonnen player must apply the effect described on the corresponding reference card at all times during the round. A ban remains active until the beginning of the next round's harvesting phase.

details.short_description: Can Stockpiling be done regardless of Imperium marker positions?
details.description: Yes. However, spice points spent on Stockpiling obviously cannot be spent on anything else. If the Supremacy marker is at step 5 or lower on the track, the Harkonnen player can spend 3 spice to earn 1 supremacy point (instead of keeping an Imperium marker in place or raising it). Maximum 1 supremacy point per harvesting phase this way.

details.short_description: Is there a difference between 'Harvester is destroyed' and 'Harvester is removed'?
details.description: No difference for game purposes. Destroyed/removed Harvesters are discarded back to the supply, but they remain available for later placement (just like Units).

details.short_description: Spice Reserve
details.description: The Harkonnen player may choose to save a maximum of 1 spice point to spend in a following round: take the spice reserve token and place it on the Spice Must Flow board as a reminder.

actions.name: End of the Round
actions.description: After Spice Harvesting, perform cleanup before starting the next round.

steps.description: The Atreides player checks whether the requirements of any revealed (end of round) prescience cards can be met. They perform any instructions found on the cards they wish to claim, and advance the prescience markers accordingly (maximum of 2 prescience cards can be claimed in one round, regardless of cards claimed during the round).
If all the scores indicated on the secret objective card are met, the Atreides player wins the game. If not, the game continues.
If any Ornithopters or Carryalls remain on the board because they were not returned earlier in the round, remove them now.
If they have named leaders on the board, starting with the Harkonnen player, both players may remove any of them from the board and deploy a generic one in place of those removed.
Flip and place back on their corresponding action boxes all 'spent' leader cards (leaders in the regeneration tank boxes are flipped face-up but not placed on the dashboards).
Both players check their hand of planning cards and discard down to 6 cards (if needed), choosing which cards to discard.

The Atreides player chooses whether to remove from the deck any revealed but unclaimed prescience cards, or reshuffles them back into the deck for the following round. Prescience cards are always discarded openly.
Both players recover their used action dice from their dashboards and a new round begins.

details.short_description: How many prescience cards can be claimed per round total?
details.description: A maximum of 2 prescience cards can be claimed in one round, regardless of when they were claimed (during the Action Resolution phase or at End of Round).

details.short_description: What happens to unclaimed prescience cards?
details.description: The Atreides player chooses whether to permanently discard any revealed but unclaimed prescience cards, or reshuffle them back into the deck for the following round. Prescience cards are always discarded openly.

details.short_description: What is the hand limit for planning cards?
details.description: Both players check their hand of planning cards and discard down to 6 cards (if needed), choosing which cards to discard.

actions.name: Action Dice: Strategy
actions.description: Move 2 different legions OR attack with 1 legion. Available to both factions.

steps.description: Move 2 different legions. You may not move the same figure twice with the same action. All movements allowed by the same action are considered simultaneous.
OR attack with 1 legion by moving it into an area containing enemy units.

notes.description: A moving legion moves from the area it occupies to an adjacent free area (containing no enemy settlements, units, or sandworms). A legion cannot move between areas separated by an impassable border, unless using troop-transport.

details.short_description: Can I move and attack with the same action?
details.description: No. Strategy lets you move 2 different legions OR attack with 1 legion. You cannot do both with a single Strategy action.

details.short_description: Are movements from the same action simultaneous?
details.description: Yes. All movements allowed by the same action are considered simultaneous. Stacking limits are only checked after all movement is complete.

actions.name: Action Dice: Leadership
actions.description: Can only be used if the affected legions contain at least 1 leader (generic or named). Move 2 different legions with a leader OR make a surprise attack with a legion with a leader.

steps.description: Move 2 different legions that each have at least one leader.
OR make a surprise attack: when making a surprise attack, the attacking player adds 1 combat die to their first round of battle only.

notes.description: If you use a Leadership action to move a legion and that legion is split, at least 1 leader must accompany the moving units.

details.short_description: When using Beast Rabban's Leader special Action, must he be part of each movement?
details.description: Yes. Rabban must be part of each movement.

details.short_description: Do Leadership actions require a leader in the legion?
details.description: Yes, the requisite of having a Leader still stands. Only Legions having a Leader must be taken into consideration for moving and attacking using a Leadership result.

actions.name: Action Dice: Deployment (Atreides Only)
actions.description: Draw a random deployment token, look at it, and place it face-down with 1 leader (Naib or named) in an area containing a sietch token.

steps.description: Draw a random Atreides deployment token from the pool.
Look at the token (secretly) and place it face-down in an area that contains a sietch token.
Place 1 Naib or named leader figure in that area.

notes.description: The Atreides player cannot take an ecological testing station if their legion is moving across multiple areas and does not stop in the station's area.

details.short_description: Can I place a deployment token in an area without a sietch?
details.description: No. Deployment tokens from this action must be placed in an area that contains a sietch token.

details.short_description: Can I look at the deployment token before placing it?
details.description: Yes. Draw the token, look at it secretly, then place it face-down in the chosen area.

actions.name: Action Dice: Mentat
actions.description: Draw 2 planning cards from your decks into your hand.

steps.description: Draw 2 planning cards: either 2 cards from the same deck or 1 from each deck (choose before drawing).

notes.description: In a 3-4 player game, the House Atreides player can also draw from the Fremen Ally deck. If they do, they must pass the card to the Fremen ally, but they can look at it before passing (without having to use a Cone of Silence).

details.short_description: Can I draw 2 cards from the same deck?
details.description: Yes. You may draw either 2 cards from the same deck or 1 from each deck (choose before drawing).

details.short_description: What if one of my decks is exhausted?
details.description: Exhausted decks are not reshuffled. If one deck is empty, you can only draw from the other. If both are empty, you draw nothing.

actions.name: Action Dice: House (Atreides)
actions.description: Use this as any action die result of your choice. This is the Atreides-specific House action.

steps.description: Treat this die as any other action die result of your choice (Strategy, Leadership, Deployment, Mentat, or Desert Power) and execute that action.

details.short_description: Can the House die be used as Desert Power?
details.description: Yes. The Atreides House action can be used as any other action die result, including Desert Power (place wormsign tokens or attack with a sandworm).

details.short_description: Does using House as Leadership still require a leader?
details.description: Yes. If you use the House die as a Leadership result, the normal requirements of that action still apply -- affected legions must contain at least 1 leader.

actions.name: Action Dice: House (Harkonnen)
actions.description: Replace 2 regular units with 2 elite units OR place 2 vehicles of your choice on the board.

steps.description: Replace 2 regular units currently on the board with 2 elite units from your supply.
OR place 2 vehicles of your choice from the supply onto Air zones on the board.

details.short_description: Can I split the House action (1 elite replacement + 1 vehicle)?
details.description: No. You must choose one option: either replace 2 regular units with 2 elite units, OR place 2 vehicles. You cannot mix the two.

details.short_description: What if I only have 1 regular unit to replace?
details.description: You can still use the action -- the effects are never mandatory and can be carried out partially or not at all.

actions.name: Movement
actions.description: Moving Legions (groups of units and leaders) across the board.

steps.description: A moving legion moves from one Area to an adjacent Area. Areas separated by impassable borders (thick red/white lines) cannot be crossed by ground movement.
You may split your legion, choosing to move only some of them and leaving behind the rest.

All areas on the board can contain a maximum of 6 units. If this limit is exceeded at any time, the controlling player must immediately remove any excess units of their choice. An area can contain any number of leaders (they are not units). An Atreides deployment token counts as 1 unit for movement and stacking limit purposes.
Leaders cannot move by themselves and must always be accompanied by at least 1 Unit.

If a legion moves across more than 1 area, it cannot pick up or drop figures during its movement. If it moves into areas that contain other friendly legions, the stacking limit is only checked after the full movement has been completed.

notes.description: Sandriding (Atreides only): An Atreides legion adjacent to an area containing a wormsign token or sandworm can move or attack across any number of adjacent, consecutive areas containing wormsign tokens or sandworms, ending their movement or attacking an enemy legion in an area adjacent to one of these wormsign tokens or sandworms. The final destination may contain a wormsign token but not a sandworm.
A sandriding legion cannot move or attack across areas containing enemy legions. Harvesters do not block sandriding.

Troop-Transport (Harkonnen only): The Harkonnen player can use 1 (only) ornithopter in an Air zone connected to the sector where a legion starts its movement. Remove the ornithopter and either move or attack with the legion up to 1 additional area away. This allows moving across impassable borders, areas containing enemy legions, and sandworms.
A legion cannot use more than 1 troop-transport per turn.

details.short_description: Can Atreides Deployment tokens be moved?
details.description: Yes. Deployment tokens can be moved in the same way as Units. However, to separate the Units contained in a Deployment token, the token must first be revealed (voluntarily or otherwise).

details.short_description: Can a Legion move and leave a Leader alone behind?
details.description: No. Leaders must always be accompanied by at least 1 Unit.

details.short_description: Can a transported Legion land in an Area not connected to the Ornithopter's Air Zone?
details.description: Yes. Only the starting Area must be connected to the Ornithopter's Air Zone.

details.short_description: Can a Legion use more than one Troop-transport in the same turn?
details.description: No, a Legion cannot use more than one Troop-transport per turn.

details.short_description: Can the Atreides combine Superior Troop-Transport with Sandriding in the same movement?
details.description: No. They must decide which 'mode' to use for each movement.

details.short_description: Can the Atreides voluntarily reveal Deployment tokens in a Sector with an Ornithopter?
details.description: The restriction only refers to the voluntary reveal from Guerrilla Training. Attacking a Legion containing a Deployment token still requires revealing it.

actions.name: Combat
actions.description: Battles occur when a Legion moves into an area containing enemy units. The battle is fought in a series of combat rounds.

steps.description: Starting with the attacking player, both players may discard planning cards from their hand to add 1 combat die to their combat roll for each discarded card.

Both players roll combat dice equal to the number of units in their legion, plus the number of planning cards they discarded. You may never roll more than 6 combat dice. If the defending legion is in an area with a settlement, in each round of the battle the defending player rolls additional combat dice equal to the settlement's rank.
Surprise Attack: when making a surprise attack (via Leadership die), the attacking player adds 1 die to their first round of battle only.
Resolve dice: each Hit inflicts 1 hit. Each Shield cancels 1 opponent's hit. Each Special may generate a Hit or Shield result, but only if leaders are involved.

Sardaukar and Fedaykin special ability: immediately before removing casualties, each Sardaukar or Fedaykin cancels 1 Shield result from the opponent's combat roll (including any Shield generated by Special results).

Remove Casualties: for each hit scored by your opponent, you must first remove 1 regular unit if possible; if there are no regular units, then you must instead replace 1 elite unit (special or not) with 1 regular unit (if available, otherwise remove the elite unit); only if there are no regular or elite units remaining may you remove 1 leader (generic or named).

If all units in a legion are eliminated, also remove any surviving leaders. All eliminated units and generic leaders become available for future deployments. Named leaders go to the regeneration tank.
Any remaining hits and shields are then discarded.

The attacking player must then decide whether or not to continue the battle. To continue, the attacker must take 1 additional hit (resolving the casualty immediately). Otherwise, the battle ends and all figures remain where they are.

notes.description: Leader Combat Abilities: each generic leader considers 1 Special result as 1 Hit. Each named leader considers 1 Special as a specific number of Hits and/or Shields, as indicated at the bottom of that leader's card.

A player whose legion includes more leaders than the number of rolled Special results must choose which leader's ability to apply for each Special. If this happens to both sides, the attacking player chooses first.
Advancing After a Battle: if the attacking player wins, the attacking legion (all Units and Leaders) CAN be moved into the attacked Area or remain where it was. The attacker is not forced to advance.
If a victorious Atreides legion advances into an area with a harvester, the harvester is removed.

Destroying Settlements: to destroy a settlement (even when undefended) you must attack it with a legion and win the battle. Special attacks (e.g., sandworms, coriolis storms, planning cards) cannot cause settlement destruction. If a settlement is undefended, the battle is won automatically by the attacker (no combat roll required).
When a sietch is destroyed, the Harkonnen player immediately gains supremacy points equal to its rank. Remove the sietch token from the board.

When a Harkonnen settlement is destroyed, the Atreides player immediately advances each prescience marker a number of steps equal to the settlement's rank: 3 for Arrakeen, 2 for Carthag, 1 for a Pyon Village. The settlement token is removed from the board.

If Arrakeen is destroyed, after advancing all Prescience markers, the Atreides player checks if they reached the score in their Secret Objective. If they did, they win immediately (instead of waiting for End of Round).

details.short_description: Can Shield results from Special Elites be cancelled?
details.description: Yes.

details.short_description: What if a player must replace an Elite with a Regular Unit, but all Regular Units are on the board?
details.description: The Elite or Special Elite is simply removed as a casualty instead of being replaced.

details.short_description: When do Named Leaders in the Regeneration Tank advance?
details.description: At the end of the Action turn.

details.short_description: Can an unspent Shield prevent a Hit when continuing battle?
details.description: No. Since this happens after adjudicating results and resolving casualties from dice, the Shield result cannot be used.

details.short_description: Do special attacks (like 'Explosive Artillery') force the reveal of a Sietch?
details.description: No.

actions.name: Deployment Tokens
actions.description: Atreides deployment tokens represent hidden units. They are a key part of the Atreides guerrilla warfare strategy.

steps.description: Atreides deployment tokens are placed face-down, hiding the combination of Regular, Elite, or Fedaykin units on their hidden side. Only the Atreides player may look at their face-down tokens.

Guerrilla Training (Free Action, Atreides only): The Atreides player can reveal any number of deployment tokens on the board. Flip the token and replace it with the corresponding units. Then remove the revealed token from the game.

Forced Reveal: Tokens must be revealed when the area is attacked by an enemy legion or when an Ornithopter scouts the area. At the beginning of a battle, if the defending legion is in an area containing an unrevealed Sietch token, flip the token face-up to reveal its rank. Also flip any deployment tokens involved in the battle, replacing them with the corresponding units.

Restriction: The Atreides cannot voluntarily reveal deployment tokens (Guerrilla Training) in a Sector with an Ornithopter in a connected Air Zone. However, tokens must still be revealed if the area is attacked.

notes.description: Deployment tokens count as 1 unit for movement and stacking limit purposes.
Deployment tokens can be moved in the same way as units. However, to separate the units inside, the token must first be revealed.

details.short_description: Can Atreides Deployment tokens be moved?
details.description: Yes. Deployment tokens can be moved in the same way as Units. However, to separate the Units contained in a Deployment token, the token must first be revealed (voluntarily or otherwise).

details.short_description: Can the Atreides voluntarily reveal tokens near an Ornithopter?
details.description: The restriction only refers to the voluntary reveal from Guerrilla Training. Attacking a Legion containing a Deployment token still requires revealing it. The Atreides cannot use Guerrilla Training in a Sector with an Ornithopter in a connected Air Zone.

actions.name: Scouting (Harkonnen)
actions.description: The Harkonnen player can use Ornithopters to scout areas, revealing hidden Atreides deployments and sietches.

steps.description: The Harkonnen player may remove 1 Ornithopter from the board to choose an area in a connected Sector.
Reveal any Sietch and all Deployment tokens in that area. Flip the sietch token face-up, and replace deployment tokens with the corresponding units (remove the deployment tokens from the game).

notes.description: Scouting is disabled when the Spacing Guild Imperium Ban is active.
Scouting costs an Ornithopter -- the Ornithopter is removed from the board and returned to the supply.

details.short_description: Does scouting cost an action die?
details.description: No. Scouting is not an action -- it costs an Ornithopter (which is removed from the board). It can be done as part of other actions.

details.short_description: What if the Spacing Guild Ban is active?
details.description: Ornithopter Scouting is disabled while the Spacing Guild Imperium Ban is active. The Harkonnen player cannot scout during this time.

actions.name: Prescience
actions.description: The Atreides player's prescience score is tracked using 3 markers: Kwisatz Haderach (green), Sand Dwellers (yellow), and Jihad (red). The prescience markers advance on the track whenever certain conditions are met.

steps.description: Claiming a Prescience card: advance the markers indicated on the card by the number written on it. Maximum 2 cards can be claimed per round. Action Resolution phase cards can only be claimed during phase 2. End of Round cards are claimed during the End of Round step.
Taking an Ecological Testing Station: advance 1 step the prescience marker whose symbol is on the back of the station's token.
Destroying a Harkonnen settlement: advance ALL prescience markers a number of steps equal to the settlement's rank (3 for Arrakeen, 2 for Carthag, 1 for a Pyon Village).

notes.description: Step 3 -- Desert Leaders: If the Jihad marker reaches this step, Chani enters play. If the Kwisatz Haderach marker reaches this step, Paul upgrades to Paul-Muad'Dib. If the Sand Dwellers marker reaches this step, Lady Jessica upgrades to Reverend Mother Jessica.
Step 6 -- Pre-born Leader: If any prescience marker reaches this step, Alia enters play.

Step 8 -- Family Atomics: If any prescience marker is on this step or higher, the Atreides player may detonate the family atomics as a free action during any of their turns. If more prescience markers reach this step after family atomics have already detonated once, there are no additional effects.

To claim a card specifying a number of conditions, the Atreides player must verify if they apply to the current circumstances. To claim a card requiring to 'spend 1 action die', the Atreides player must spend 1 unused action die as their action for the turn, forfeiting its corresponding actions.

details.short_description: When can 'Families Flee to the Palmaries of the South' be claimed?
details.description: As a Free Action during the Atreides' turn.

details.short_description: Does the Prescience card with 'Eyes of Ibad' remain in play?
details.description: Until it is claimed, that Prescience card remains in play. So, on the following rounds, it is a fourth available Prescience card.

details.short_description: What happens to claimed Prescience cards?
details.description: They are effectively out of the game, though we suggest laying them out next to the Atreides dashboard as a thematic display of the Atreides journey.

actions.name: Named Leader Special Actions
actions.description: Each named leader card is associated with a special action die result. Place a named leader card that enters play face-up on your dashboard, on the action box with the corresponding action symbol.

steps.description: Once you have fully activated a leader's special action, pull the leader card out of the dashboard and flip it face-down. That card is now spent and its special action is not available until the beginning of the following round.

Unless they are in the regeneration tank, leaders allow you to choose from additional or enhanced options (in green for Atreides, and in red for Harkonnen) when spending a matching die result to execute an action. You can always choose between a regular action and a special one.

notes.description: Jessica and Paul: If Reverend Mother Jessica enters play, her figure and card immediately replace those of Lady Jessica, who are removed from the game. They are placed in the same position as the replaced ones: either on the board, set aside, or on the regeneration tank. The same applies when Paul-Muad'Dib enters play -- the figure and card of the replaced Paul Atreides are removed from the game.

If a planning or prescience card refers to Paul, it is applicable to both Paul Atreides or Paul-Muad'Dib. If a card refers to Jessica, it is applicable to both Lady Jessica or Reverend Mother Jessica.

Thufir Hawat and Gaius Helen Mohiam: If Thufir Hawat is in play, the Harkonnen player can remove him permanently from the game by discarding 2 or 3 Hawat's Scheming planning cards from their hand as a free action. As soon as Thufir Hawat is removed from the game, Gaius Helen Mohiam enters play.
If Thufir Hawat has been removed from the game and the Harkonnen player has a Hawat's Scheming card in their hand, they can no longer discard it.
Beast Rabban and Feyd-Rautha: If Feyd-Rautha enters play, Beast Rabban is immediately removed from the game (remove his card and figure).

Gurney Halleck: When Gurney Halleck first enters play, the Atreides player places his figure directly on the board. Gurney Halleck is an Atreides leader who has no leader abilities and no special actions.

Wild Maker: This giant sandworm enters play if the Atreides player plays 1 of the 3 Sand-Hulud Fremen Ally planning cards. It is placed instead of a sandworm for all rules and effects. It can be placed on the board, moved, used for sandriding, or to attack like any other sandworm (though its attacks are more lethal -- 6 combat dice instead of 4).

details.short_description: When do spent leader cards refresh?
details.description: At the End of Round, flip and place back on their corresponding action boxes all 'spent' leader cards. Leaders in the regeneration tank are flipped face-up but not placed on dashboards.

details.short_description: Can a named leader's special action be used while in the Regeneration Tank?
details.description: No. Unless they are in the regeneration tank, leaders allow you to choose from additional or enhanced options when spending a matching die result.

details.short_description: What happens when Paul upgrades to Paul-Muad'Dib?
details.description: The figure and card of Paul Atreides are removed from the game entirely. Paul-Muad'Dib is placed in the same position (board, set aside, or regeneration tank). Any card referring to 'Paul' applies to both versions.

end_game.trigger: The game ends immediately when either victory condition is met, or at the end of a round when checked.
procedure: Check after each round's End of Round step (after Spice Harvesting).
Harkonnen victory: 10 or more Supremacy Points on the Supremacy Track (immediate win when destroying a Sietch pushes SP to 10+).
Atreides victory: all three Prescience markers have reached or exceeded the thresholds shown on the Atreides player's secret Objective card.
Special case: If Arrakeen is destroyed, after advancing all Prescience markers, the Atreides player checks immediately (not at End of Round). If thresholds are met, Atreides wins immediately.

final_scoring.description: Gained by destroying Fremen Sietches (points equal to sietch rank). Also 1 SP per round from spending 3 spice during harvesting (only if Supremacy marker is at step 5 or below). 10+ SP wins the game.
Three tracks: Kwisatz Haderach (green), Sand Dwellers (yellow), and Jihad (red). Must reach thresholds on ALL three tracks as defined by the secret Objective card.

winning_condition: There is no tiebreaker -- each faction has a distinct win condition. Harkonnen: 10+ Supremacy Points. Atreides: meet all three Prescience thresholds. If neither condition is met, the game continues to the next round.

tips_for_new_players.description: Harkonnen has raw military power (more units, more dice, vehicles). Atreides has flexibility (Desert Power, Sandriding, more leaders, hidden deployment). Play to your faction's strengths.

You need to destroy Sietches for victory BUT also harvest spice to maintain Imperium relations. Neglecting spice leads to Imperium Bans that cripple your capabilities. It costs 2 spice to keep a marker in place and 3 to raise it.
Don't try to match Harkonnen in direct combat. Use hit-and-fade tactics, Sandriding for mobility, and focus on advancing Prescience tracks rather than holding territory.

The Atreides player should leverage Desert Power (free action when you have fewer unused dice). This partially compensates for having only 4 dice vs Harkonnen's 8. Use it to place wormsign tokens or activate sandworms.

Atreides: each destroyed Sietch brings Harkonnen closer to victory. Defend or keep them hidden using deployment tokens. Harkonnen: identifying and attacking Sietches is your primary path to victory -- use Ornithopter Scouting to reveal them.

Leaders provide combat abilities (converting Special die results) and enable Leadership actions. Named leaders have unique powers on their Leader cards. Eliminated named leaders go to the Regeneration Tank and take several turns to return -- use them wisely.

Mountain areas are safe from sandworms and Coriolis Storms. Desert areas are dangerous (Deep Desert doubly so -- Special results deal 2 hits in storms). Use terrain to your advantage -- Atreides can weaponize the desert, while Harkonnen should be wary of overextending.

Steps 3, 6, and 8 on the Prescience track trigger powerful effects: Step 3 upgrades Paul and Jessica, Step 6 brings Alia into play, and Step 8 unlocks Family Atomics. Plan your prescience advancement strategically.

Each unit contributes 1 combat die (max 6). Generic leaders convert Special results to 1 Hit. Named leaders have unique and often stronger conversions. Sardaukar and Fedaykin each cancel 1 enemy Shield before casualties -- they are extremely valuable.

An area is free when it has no enemy Settlements, Units, or Sandworms. Harvesters and Ecological Stations don't make an area non-free. To destroy a settlement, you must attack with a legion -- special attacks like sandworms cannot destroy settlements.

All Harkonnen vehicles are returned at end of round. Plan vehicle placement based on that round's priorities -- they don't carry over. Use Ornithopters for Scouting and Troop-Transport, Harvesters for spice, and Carryalls to protect Harvesters from sandworms.

The Action Resolution phase ends when the Harkonnen player has used all their available Actions. The Atreides player loses any remaining Actions. Atreides should try to use their most important actions early.
